description_long: This house is a low-rise, rectangular residential structure composed of multiple connected box-like volumes, each with flat roofs. The exterior is primarily a light, off-white color with some areas showing brownish discoloration, possibly from weathering or material variation. Windows and doors are distributed across the facades, with some sections featuring small protrusions or utility elements. The roofline is mostly flat, with a few minor height variations and rooftop equipment visible. The building appears to be a single story, with a utilitarian, modernist design and minimal decorative features. The overall appearance suggests a practical, functional home, possibly in a suburban or urban neighborhood.
description: A single-story, flat-roofed house with a boxy, modernist design and light-colored exterior, featuring scattered windows and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: This view shows the house from a short end, highlighting the boxy modules and some rooftop equipment.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the long, linear arrangement of the house, with multiple windows and flat roof sections.
description_view_2: This end view emphasizes the depth of the house and shows a few utility features and protrusions.
description_view_3: This opposite side view displays the extended length of the house and the arrangement of windows and roof elements.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main door with minimal steps, no porch or columns, and a flat overhang.
